relationships.description: ISO 42005 AI impact assessment process implements ISO 31000 Risk Assessment for AI-specific societal, individual, and environmental impacts.
ISO 42005 impact treatment implements ISO 31000 Risk Treatment for mitigating negative AI impacts and enhancing positive ones.
ISO 42005 impact assessment process supports ISO 42001 Clause 8.4 (AI system impact assessment). ISO 42005 provides the methodology that ISO 42001 requires.
ISO 42005 human rights impact dimension implements EU AI Act Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (Article 27).
ISO 42005 human rights impact assessment relates to GDPR DPIA. AI systems processing personal data require both AI impact and privacy impact assessments.
ISO 42005 stakeholder engagement relates to NIST AI RMF Map function for understanding affected parties and deployment context.
ISO 42005 vulnerable groups consideration relates to EU AI Act prohibited practices. Both address protection of vulnerable populations from harmful AI.
ISO 42005 monitoring and review maps to ISO 23894 monitoring and review. Both require continuous monitoring of AI impacts and risks.
ISO 42005 lifecycle integration maps to NIST AI RMF lifecycle stages for impact assessment throughout the AI system lifecycle.
ISO 42005 environmental impacts relate to EU AI Act GPAI systemic risk. Large-scale AI models have significant environmental impacts that factor into systemic risk assessment.
ISO 42005 governance and accountability dimension relates to ISO 42001 Leadership clause for organizational accountability in AI governance.
